resources.string: Error loading programs. Please try again.
No workouts yet. Add a new one!
No profile selected. Please select or create a profile in settings.
Error loading workouts. Please try again.
How many sets will you do?
Set your targets for this exercise:
At least one muscle group has to be selected
At least one equipment has to be selected
At least one recommendation has to be selected
Wall Ball Squat and Press (No Throw)
Kneel on a mat, hold ab wheel handles. Slowly roll wheel forward, extending body. Use abs to pull back to start.
Stand with feet hip-width. Bend and place ab wheel on floor. Roll forward into a plank, then extend further if possible. Pull back to start. (Advanced)
Adjust seat height. Grip handles and place feet on pedals. Pedal and push/pull handles simultaneously. Maintain a consistent pace for endurance or perform intervals for high intensity.
Start on all fours, ankle weights on. Keep one knee bent at 90 degrees. Lift that leg up towards ceiling, foot flat, squeezing glute at the top. Lower with control. Repeat and switch legs.
Lie on your back, ankle weights on. Perform leg raises as usual (lying, hanging, or captain's chair), focusing on controlling the added weight through the movement. Works hip flexors and lower abs.
Lie on back, ankle weights on. Knees bent. Pull knees to chest, lifting hips, using lower abs. Lower slowly.

Lie on a flat bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing up. Start with arms extended by your hips. In a circular motion, bring the dumbbells up and around until they meet above your chest. Reverse the motion to return to the start.

Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a weight plate with both hands. Keeping your arms straight, raise the plate in a large circular motion around your head. Complete reps in one direction, then switch.

Guide for assisted pull-ups. Use a resistance band looped over the pull-up bar and under your feet or knees, or use an assisted pull-up machine. Perform the pull-up motion, focusing on good form. The assistance helps complete reps.
Stand with feet shoulder-width, holding one end of rope in each hand. Create waves by rapidly moving arms up and down, alternating or together. Keep core engaged, slight bend in knees.

Lie on bench, feet flat on floor, eyes under bar. Grip bar slightly wider than shoulder-width. Arch lower back slightly, retract shoulder blades. Unrack bar. Lower to mid-chest, elbows tucked slightly (not flared). Press bar up until arms extended. Repeat.
Position bench inside power rack. Set J-hooks for unracking bar. Set safety pins just above chest height when lying down. Perform barbell bench press as usual, with added safety from pins.

Lie on bench, feet flat. Hold dumbbells above chest, palms facing forward or each other. Lower dumbbells to sides of chest, elbows slightly tucked. Press back up to start position. Control the movement throughout.
Position bench under Smith machine bar. Lie down, align chest with bar. Grip bar, unhook. Lower bar to chest, then press up. The machine guides the path. Focus on muscle contraction.
Lie on back, hands behind head. Bring one knee to opposite elbow, alternating sides in a pedaling motion.

Perform box jumps as usual, but with a conscious effort to maximize the push-off from your calves during the jump. Focus on explosive plantar flexion (pointing toes down) as you leave the ground. Land softly.

Stand facing a plyo box a comfortable distance away. Swing arms back, then forward explosively as you jump onto the center of the box. Land softly in a partial squat position with both feet fully on the box. Stand up straight on the box, then step down carefully one foot at a time. Do not jump down.

Start standing. Drop into a squat, place hands on floor. Kick feet back into a plank. Optional: do a push-up. Jump feet back to squat position. Jump up explosively, reaching arms overhead. Land softly. Repeat.
Kneel facing a high cable pulley with rope attachment. Hold rope by head. Crunch down, pulling elbows towards knees.
Stand facing a high cable pulley with rope/bar. Hold attachment by head/chest. Crunch forward, engaging abs.

Place a barbell across your upper back/traps (like a squat). Stand with feet hip-width apart, balls of feet potentially on an elevated surface. Rise up onto the balls of your feet, lifting heels. Squeeze calves. Slowly lower heels. Maintain balance and control.

Sit on a bench or chair with your knees bent at a 90-degree angle and feet flat on the floor. Place your hands on your thighs for slight resistance if desired, or let them rest. Lift your heels off the floor as high as possible by pressing through the balls of your feet. Squeeze your calves. Slowly lower your heels. This targets the soleus muscle more.

Stand with feet hip-width apart, toes pointing forward. You can place the balls of your feet on an elevated surface (like a step or plate) for a greater range of motion. Slowly rise up onto the balls of your feet, lifting your heels as high as possible. Squeeze your calves at the top. Slowly lower your heels back down. Repeat.

Sit on a bench, knees bent at 90 degrees, feet flat. Place a dumbbell vertically on each thigh, near the knee, holding them in place. Press up through the balls of your feet, lifting heels high. Squeeze calves. Slowly lower heels. This emphasizes the soleus.

Hold a dumbbell in each hand by your sides. Stand with feet hip-width apart. Rise up onto the balls of your feet, lifting heels high. Squeeze calves. Slowly lower heels. For more range, stand with balls of feet on an elevated surface.

Stand on one leg, holding onto a wall or sturdy object for balance if needed. You can place the ball of your working foot on an elevated surface. Lift the heel of your working leg as high as possible, rising onto the ball of your foot. Squeeze your calf. Slowly lower your heel. Complete reps for one leg, then switch.

Stand on one leg, holding a dumbbell in the hand on the same side as your working leg (other hand can be used for balance). Ball of working foot can be on an elevated surface. Rise up onto the ball of your foot, lifting heel. Squeeze calf. Slowly lower. Complete reps, then switch leg and dumbbell hand.

Position yourself under the Smith machine bar, bar across upper back. Feet hip-width apart, balls of feet optionally on an elevated block. Unrack the bar. Rise up onto the balls of your feet, pushing through your toes. Squeeze calves at the top. Slowly lower heels. The machine provides stability.
Support forearms on pads of a captain\'s chair/dip station, back against pad. Raise knees or straight legs up.

Adjust seat so handles are mid-chest level. Sit with back flat against pad. Grip handles. Press handles forward until arms are extended but not locked. Slowly return to start position, feeling stretch in chest.
Lie on your back, knees bent, feet flat. Place hands behind head or across chest. Curl upper body towards knees. Lower slowly.
Lie on your back, knees bent. Hold a weight plate on your chest or behind your head. Curl upper body towards knees.

Stand with mid-foot under barbell. Feet hip-width apart. Bend at hips and knees to grip bar, hands shoulder-width apart. Keep back straight, chest up, core braced. Lift by extending hips and knees, keeping bar close to body. Lower by reversing motion, maintaining flat back.
Lie on a decline bench, securing feet. Hands behind head or across chest. Curl upper body up. Lower slowly.

Place your feet on top of a plyo box (or elevated surface) and hands on the floor shoulder-width apart, creating a decline angle for your body. Keep your body straight from head to heels. Lower your chest towards the floor by bending your elbows. Push back up to the starting position. Keep core engaged throughout.

Support yourself on dip bars (or captain's chair). Keeping legs straight (or knees bent for easier version), raise legs up in front of you as high as possible. Lower slowly with control. Focus on lower abs.
Stand tall, hold one dumbbell in one hand. Bend sideways at the waist towards the dumbbell. Return and repeat. Switch sides.
Lie on bench. Grip EZ-bar with close overhand grip on inner angled sections. Lower bar to lower chest, keeping elbows tucked close to body. Press bar up, focusing on triceps and inner chest.

Hold EZ-curl bar with underhand grip on angled sections, hands shoulder-width or slightly narrower. Stand tall, elbows pinned to sides. Curl bar up towards shoulders, squeezing biceps. Lower slowly with control until arms are fully extended.

Sit at preacher bench, upper arms resting on pad. Hold EZ-bar with underhand grip. Curl bar up towards shoulders. Squeeze biceps at top. Lower bar slowly until arms are almost fully extended, maintaining tension.
Hold EZ-curl bar with overhand grip (palms down) on angled sections. Stand tall, elbows pinned to sides. Curl bar up towards shoulders, focusing on forearms and brachialis. Lower slowly with control.

Lie on bench, hold EZ-bar above chest with overhand grip, narrower than shoulder-width. Keeping upper arms perpendicular to body, bend elbows to lower bar towards forehead/behind head. Extend arms to press bar back up, focusing on triceps.

Hold EZ-bar with overhand grip, hands close together on inner angled sections. Stand tall. Pull bar straight up towards chin, leading with elbows. Keep bar close to body. Lower slowly. Focus on shoulders and traps.
Lie on back, legs straight, hands under glutes. Lift heels slightly. Perform small, quick up-and-down scissor-like kicks.

Hold one kettlebell by the horns (or one end of a dumbbell vertically) against your chest. Feet slightly wider than shoulder-width. Squat down, keeping chest up, elbows tracking inside knees. Go as low as possible. Drive up through heels.
Hang from a pull-up bar. Bend knees and raise them towards your chest. Lower slowly.
Hang from a pull-up bar. Keeping legs straight, raise them as high as possible. Lower slowly.
Lie on back, knees bent, feet flat. Lift shoulders slightly. Reach to touch right heel with right hand, then left heel with left hand. Alternate.
Lie on an incline bench. Grip the barbell with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width. Lower the bar to your upper chest, then press it back up until your arms are fully extended.
Lie on an incline bench holding a dumbbell in each hand. Press the dumbbells up until your arms are fully extended. Lower them back down to the sides of your upper chest.
Lie on an incline bench, head at higher end, gripping bench. Knees bent. Pull knees to chest, lifting hips.

Hold jump rope handles with a relaxed grip, elbows close to your body. Swing the rope using your wrists, not your whole arms. Jump just high enough for the rope to clear under your feet. Land softly on the balls of your feet. Maintain a steady rhythm.
Stand with feet together, hands by sides. Jump, spreading feet wide while bringing arms overhead. Jump again to return to starting position. Repeat in a continuous rhythm.

Start with KB on floor between feet. Clean KB to shoulder rack position in one fluid motion (hike, pull, catch). From rack, press KB overhead. Lower KB to rack, then back to floor or into next clean. One side at a time or alternate.

Stand with feet hip/shoulder-width apart, kettlebell between feet. Hinge at hips, keeping back straight, grip KB handle. Drive through heels, extend hips and knees to lift KB. Squeeze glutes at top. Lower with control.
Hold one heavy kettlebell in each hand (or one if performing single-sided). Stand tall, shoulders back, core braced. Walk for a set distance or time, maintaining posture and control. Avoid leaning.
Stand holding kettlebell by horns, upside down, at chest level. Circle the kettlebell around your head, keeping it close. Alternate directions. Engage core to stabilize.
Hold kettlebell(s) in desired position (e.g., goblet style, farmer's carry, racked). Perform lunges as per bodyweight/dumbbell lunge instructions. Maintain balance and core stability.

Sit on floor, lean back slightly, knees bent, feet off floor (or on floor for stability). Hold KB with both hands at chest. Twist torso from side to side, touching KB (or near) floor on each side. Keep core engaged.

Place one hand and knee on bench (or hinge at hips, supporting with one hand on a surface). Hold kettlebell in other hand, arm extended. Pull KB up towards hip, squeezing back. Lower with control. Keep back straight.

Start with KB between feet. Hike pass, then explosively extend hips, knees, ankles, pulling KB up. As KB reaches chest height, punch hand through and catch it overhead in one smooth motion. Lower with control or drop into swing. Advanced.

Stand with feet slightly wider than shoulders, kettlebell in front. Hinge at hips, grip KB with both hands. Hike KB back between legs, then thrust hips forward powerfully, swinging KB to chest height. Let KB swing back down with control. Movement is hip-driven, not arms.

Lie down holding KB in one hand straight up. Get up to standing position, keeping KB overhead and stable, then reverse to lie down. Involves multiple steps: roll to elbow, to hand, lift hips, sweep leg, lunge to stand. Complex, learn steps.
Feet shoulder-width, one KB overhead. Hinge at hip of non-KB side, keeping KB-arm straight up, eyes on KB. Lower torso towards floor, other hand traces down leg. Return to start. Switch sides.
Barbell in landmine attachment. Hold end of barbell with both hands, arms extended. Rotate torso side to side, moving barbell in an arc.
Place one end of barbell in a sturdy corner. Hold other end with both hands, arms extended. Rotate torso side to side.

Place barbell on upper back (like a squat). Perform lunges, stepping forward one leg at a time. Maintain balance and control. Keep front knee over ankle, back knee near floor. Push back to start. Alternate.

Stand tall. Step forward with one leg, lowering hips until both knees are bent at a 90-degree angle. Front knee over ankle, back knee just above floor. Push off front foot to return to start. Alternate legs.
Hold dumbbells by your sides. Step forward with one leg, performing a lunge as per bodyweight lunge instructions. Keep torso upright. Push off front foot to return. Alternate legs.
Lie flat on your back, legs straight. Hands under glutes or by sides. Raise legs until perpendicular to floor. Lower slowly.
Lie on back, hold a dumbbell securely between feet. Raise legs. Lower slowly, keeping control.

Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a medicine ball with both hands. Extend arms overhead, slightly arching your back. Explosively slam the ball down onto the ground in front of you, using your core and hinging at your hips. Squat down to pick up the ball (or catch it on the bounce if it’s a bouncy medicine ball). Repeat.
Sit on floor, lean back slightly, knees bent, feet off floor (or on floor for stability). Hold medicine ball at chest. Twist torso side to side, touching ball (or near) floor on each side.

Stand with feet shoulder-width, hold medicine ball overhead. Explosively slam ball to ground just in front of you, using core and hinging at hips. Catch ball on bounce (if applicable) or pick up. Repeat.

Stand with feet shoulder-width, hold medicine ball. Start with ball at one shoulder. Chop diagonally across body towards opposite knee/foot, rotating torso. Control return to start. Alternate sides/directions (high-to-low, low-to-high).

Start in a plank position. Drive one knee towards your chest, then quickly switch and drive the other knee in. Continue alternating legs rapidly as if running in place while in plank. Keep core tight, hips low.

Guide for negative pull-ups. Start at the top position of a pull-up (chin over bar), either by jumping or using a bench. Slowly lower your body, controlling the descent for as long as possible (aim for 3-5 seconds). Fully extend arms at the bottom. Repeat.

Stand with bar at front shoulder level, grip slightly wider than shoulders. Core braced. Press bar directly overhead until arms fully extended. Lower bar under control back to shoulders. Avoid leaning back excessively.
Set barbell on J-hooks in power rack at shoulder height. Perform overhead press as usual, standing or seated. The rack can provide safety if pins are set or help with getting the bar into position.
Sit or stand, hold dumbbells at shoulder height, palms forward. Core braced. Press dumbbells overhead until arms are fully extended. Lower dumbbells slowly back to shoulder height.
Sit or stand under Smith machine bar. Position bar at shoulder level. Grip bar, unhook. Press bar overhead. Lower with control. Path is fixed by machine.

Lie face down. Place forearms on floor, elbows under shoulders, hands clasped or flat. Lift hips so body forms a straight line from head to heels. Engage core and glutes. Hold position. Don\'t let hips sag or rise too high.

Stand holding a weight plate with both hands, arms extended downwards. Keeping arms straight (slight bend in elbow is okay), raise the plate in front of you up to shoulder height. Lower slowly with control. Focus on front deltoids.

Hold a weight plate with both hands at chest level. Circle the plate around your head, keeping core engaged and movement controlled. Alternate directions. Good for shoulder mobility and core stability.
Stand facing plyo box. Swing arms and jump explosively onto center of box, landing softly in a squat position. Stand up on box. Step down (don't jump down). Repeat.
Stand facing plyo box. Step up onto box with one foot, driving through heel. Bring other foot up to stand on box. Step down with lead foot, then other. Alternate lead foot.

Grip pull-up bar with overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulders. Hang with arms fully extended. Pull your body up until chin is over the bar. Squeeze back muscles. Lower yourself slowly until arms are fully extended. Repeat.

Start in a plank position, hands shoulder-width apart, body straight from head to heels. Lower your body by bending elbows, keeping them close to body or slightly flared, until chest nearly touches floor. Push back up to start. Keep core engaged.

Grip push-up handles placed shoulder-width apart. Assume plank position. Lower body by bending elbows, aiming for deeper range of motion than standard push-ups. Keep core engaged and body straight. Push back up to start.

Set barbell on safety pins in power rack at desired height (e.g., just below knees). Stand with mid-foot under bar. Perform top portion of deadlift: hinge at hips, grip bar, keep back straight, lift by extending hips and standing tall. Lower with control.
Lie on back, knees bent at 90 degrees. Hands by sides or under head. Pull knees towards chest, lifting hips off floor. Lower slowly.

Hold barbell in front of thighs, overhand grip. Slight knee bend. Hinge at hips, lowering bar by pushing hips back, keeping back straight and bar close to legs. Lower until stretch in hamstrings or bar is mid-shin. Squeeze glutes to return to upright position.

Hold dumbbells in front of thighs, palms facing body. Feet hip-width. Slight bend in knees. Hinge at hips, lowering dumbbells towards floor, keeping back straight and dumbbells close to legs. Feel stretch in hamstrings. Return to start by squeezing glutes.

Secure feet in straps. Start with legs bent, arms extended holding handle. Drive with legs, then lean back slightly with core, finally pull handle to lower chest/upper abs. Reverse order: arms, then core, then legs. Repeat.
Hug the sandbag tightly against your chest. Feet shoulder-width apart. Perform a squat, keeping the sandbag secure and torso as upright as possible. This heavily engages the upper back and core.
Pick up sandbag (e.g., bear hug, shoulder, zercher). Stand tall, core braced. Walk for distance or time, maintaining posture and control. Various carry positions work muscles differently.

Start with sandbag on floor. Explosively lift (clean) sandbag to shoulder or chest level. From there, press sandbag overhead. Lower with control to shoulder, then to floor. More rugged than barbell version.

Stand with feet hip-width apart, sandbag on the floor in front of you (or lengthwise between your feet). Grip the sandbag firmly. In one explosive movement, drive with your hips and legs, shrug your shoulders, and pull the sandbag up towards your shoulders. Quickly rotate your elbows under the bag to catch it in a front rack position (on your shoulders/chest). Stand tall. Lower the bag back to the floor with control. Repeat.
Hold sandbag (e.g., on one shoulder, zercher). Perform lunges, stepping forward and maintaining balance. The shifting nature of the sandbag challenges stability. Alternate legs/sandbag position.

Clean the sandbag to one shoulder. Brace your core. Press the sandbag directly overhead, fully extending your arm. Lower the sandbag with control to the opposite shoulder. Press it overhead from that shoulder. Continue alternating shoulders for each press. Maintain stability throughout.
Hold sandbag in desired position (e.g., front-loaded/zercher, on back, bear hug). Perform squats as per standard squat form, focusing on maintaining balance and control with the unstable load.

Adjust seat and chest pad. Sit facing machine, feet on footrests. Grip handles with desired grip (narrow/wide). Pull handles towards your torso, squeezing shoulder blades together. Keep back straight. Slowly extend arms to return.
Stand with feet together. Step out to one side, keeping other leg straight. Bend knee of stepping leg and sit hips back. Keep chest up. Push off stepping foot to return to center. Alternate sides.
Hold dumbbells (e.g., at shoulders or by sides). Perform side lunges as per bodyweight instructions, stepping out to one side and sitting hips back. Maintain control. Alternate sides.

Lie on back, knees bent, feet flat (can be anchored). Hands behind head or across chest. Curl your torso up towards your knees until you are in a seated position. Slowly lower back down. Focus on using abs, not momentum.
Lie on back, knees bent, feet anchored. Hold a dumbbell on chest or behind head. Sit up fully. Lower slowly.
Lie on back, knees bent, feet anchored. Hold a weight plate on chest. Sit up fully. Lower slowly.

Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, with the slam ball on the ground between your feet. Squat down with a straight back and pick up the ball. Explosively lift the ball straight overhead, fully extending your body (hips, knees, and ankles). Control the ball as you lower it back to the ground for the next repetition. This is not a throw.

Stand with feet shoulder-width, slam ball on floor between feet. Squat to pick it up. Explosively extend hips and knees, tossing ball back over one shoulder. Turn, pick up, repeat over other shoulder. Alternate.

Position barbell on upper back/traps, not neck. Feet shoulder-width apart. Follow bodyweight squat form: hips back and down, chest up, core tight, knees tracking with toes. Descend to desired depth. Drive up through heels.
Set barbell on J-hooks in power rack at shoulder height. Set safety pins below squat depth. Perform barbell squat as usual, using rack for safety and easier racking/unracking.

Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, chest up, core engaged. Lower your hips back and down as if sitting in a chair, keeping knees behind toes. Go as low as comfortable, ideally thighs parallel to the floor. Push through heels to return to start.

Hold dumbbells at shoulders or by your sides. Feet shoulder-width apart. Perform a squat: hips back and down, chest up, core engaged, knees tracking with toes. Lower to comfortable depth. Push through heels to stand.

Position yourself under Smith machine bar, bar across upper back. Unrack bar. Feet shoulder-width. Perform squat, path is fixed. Focus on form, pushing hips back. Descend to desired depth, then press up.
Select speed and incline. Start walking, then increase to running pace. Maintain good posture, land mid-foot or forefoot. Use handrails for balance if needed, but try to swing arms naturally.
Select speed and incline. Walk at a steady pace. Can be used for warm-up, cool-down, or active recovery. Maintain good posture.

Grip parallel dip bars, support body with arms straight. Lean torso slightly forward for chest focus, or keep more upright for triceps. Lower body by bending elbows until upper arms are parallel to floor or comfortable depth. Push back up to start.

Stand facing a wall, a few feet away, holding wall ball at chest in squat position. Explosively stand and throw ball to a target high on wall. Catch ball as it descends, immediately going into next squat. Repeat.

Hold a wall ball (or medicine ball) at chest height. Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Perform a full squat, keeping your chest up and core engaged. As you stand up from the squat, press the ball overhead, fully extending your arms. Lower the ball back to chest height as you descend into the next squat. The ball is not thrown against a wall in this variation.
Perform dips as above, but with added weight (e.g., dip belt with plates, weighted vest). Focus on maintaining form with the extra load.

Get into a standard forearm or hand plank position. Have a partner carefully place a weight plate on your upper/mid back. Hold the plank, maintaining straight body line and engaged core. Partner removes plate before you finish.
Stand sideways to a high cable pulley with handle. Grip handle with both hands. Pull diagonally down across body.
Stand with feet shoulder-width, hold one dumbbell with both hands above one shoulder. Chop diagonally down across body. Control return.
Stand with feet shoulder-width, hold kettlebell by horns above one shoulder. Chop diagonally down. Control return.
Wear wrist weights. Stand with arms extended out to sides at shoulder height. Make small controlled circles forward for a set time/reps, then backward. Works shoulder endurance.
Wear wrist weights. Assume boxing stance. Throw punches (jabs, crosses, hooks, uppercuts) with good form, focusing on speed and control against the added resistance. Keep core engaged.
